title: tounatea dodecandra
intro: Tounatea dodecandra, also known as Ipeca, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, belonging to the Ipecac family. The medicinal properties of Tounatea dodecandra lie in its roots, which contain the alkaloid emetine, a powerful emetic and expectorant. This plant is used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to stimulate coughing and bring up phlegm. Tounatea dodecandra can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tinctures, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to relieve symptoms of respiratory ailments.
